launch_mission_workflow with config.mission_id=epic_id.

commit.message:
fix(upload): skip terraform_data curl upload if image already Active

When harvester-vm.sh's Python3 uploader completes before terraform_data
reaches its curl upload, the server closes the connection with a
PROTOCOL_ERROR (the upload action is no longer available on an Active image).

Add a final Active-state check right before the curl upload so that if an
external upload already succeeded the terraform_data provisioner exits 0
instead of attempting a redundant upload.

commit.message:
fix(backend): server starts immediately, DB retries in background

Previously: server only started after connectDB() succeeded — so any
DB hiccup (Supabase paused, network, bad creds) crashed the whole
process and Render would restart-loop endlessly.

Now:
- server.listen() fires immediately on startup
- connectDB() runs non-blocking with exponential backoff (5s→60s)
- /api/health responds even while DB is reconnecting
- Once Supabase is reachable the pool heals automatically

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(watchdog): import _run_workflow_background from correct module

_run_workflow_background lives in web.routes.workflows, not epics.execution.
Wrong import caused watchdog to fail when relaunching stuck AC cycles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(api): remove unsafe type assertions and non-null operators

L-02: Replace 3 `as unknown as` patterns with proper type narrowing
- orders/status: runtime type guard for RPC result instead of cast
- batch-user-emails: remove redundant cast (RPC already typed)
- EnrichmentQueue: use `in` operator to narrow union type

L-03: Replace 3 non-null assertions in IntegrationService
- Add explicit null guard for integrationResult.data after success check

L-04: Replace 3 `as string` casts in KeywordEnrichmentService
- Add isJobCancelled() helper to avoid TypeScript control-flow narrowing
- Remove `as string` on enum status comparisons

fix: load MCP servers from ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json for CLI parity

Sessions created via the dashboard had no MCP servers, even though
~/.copilot/mcp-config.json was configured with WorkIQ and Obsidian.
The SDK doesn't auto-load this file in shared server mode.

Now CreateSession reads ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json and passes the
servers via SessionConfig.MCPServers, giving dashboard sessions the
same MCP tools as the CLI.

commit.message:
Add exponential backoff recovery to PollingAgent

Replace single-attempt session recovery with 5-attempt exponential
backoff (2s → 4s → 8s → 16s → 30s cap), matching the TUI
SessionManager pattern.

- RecoverSessionWithBackoffAsync: new method with retry loop
- RunLoopAsync: tracks consecutive failures, adaptive polling
  interval (normal × 2^failures, capped at 5 min), 60s cooldown
  after total recovery failure
- SendMessageAsync: catches communication errors, recovers with
  backoff, and retries the message once

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(backend): robust URL parser — split on last @ to handle password with @

pg-connection-string uses Node's url.parse which splits on the FIRST @,
mangling passwords that contain @ (encoded or raw).

Replace connectionString Pool config with a manual parser that:
- Finds the LAST @ as the user/host boundary
- Calls decodeURIComponent on the password (handles both raw @ and %40)
- Passes individual params to pg.Pool, bypassing url.parse entirely

Works with both Render dashboard URL (raw @) and .env URL (%40).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove locale:missing; standardise on locale:download:missing (#8204)

The local-diff approach (`locale-build-missing.js` / `locale:missing`)
was redundant — POEditor's own `filters=untranslated` export API already
computes missing terms server-side with no prior full download required.
This removes the old script and wires everything to
`locale:download:missing`.

## Changes

- **Deleted** `locale/scripts/locale-build-missing.js`
- **`package.json`** — removed `locale:missing` script
- **`locale/scripts/locale-translate.js`** — updated fallback hint to
`locale:download:missing`
- **`locale/scripts/poeditor-missing-downloader.js`** — cleaned up JSDoc
(dropped cross-references to deleted file)
- **`locale/README.md`** — collapsed two-option workflow to single
canonical approach; removed `locale:missing` from scripts table and
quick-reference block
- **`.github/workflows/locale-poeditor-download.yml`** — replaced `npm
run locale:missing` step with `npm run locale:download:missing`; updated
commit step paths from `locale/missing-terms/poeditor-*.json` →
`locale/terms/missing/`
- **`.agents/skills/churchcrm/locale-ai-translation.md`** and
**`.claude/commands/locale-release.md`** — Quick Start and scripts table
updated to `locale:download:missing`

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

💡 You can make Copilot smarter by setting up custom instructions,
customizing its development environment and configuring Model Context
Protocol (MCP) servers. Learn more [Copilot coding agent
tips](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips) in the docs.

---------

- L-09: Add indexnow-net custom bridge network to all Docker services
- L-12: Add in-memory TTL cache (1h, max 10k entries) for IP geolocation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(upload): fix IMAGE_SIZE bash syntax error in terraform_data heredoc

$$(wc...) expands $$ to the shell PID then makes (wc...) an unexpected
token — syntax error. Terraform only interpolates ${...}, not $(...),
so a plain $(wc...) passes through to bash as valid command substitution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[identity] Terms Acceptance Requirement fix. Ensure user claims are loaded before terms check (#945)

* [identity] Ensure user claims are loaded before terms check

Load user claims if missing before validating terms acceptance.
Update logic to require acceptance only if terms were modified
in the past and user's acceptance predates the modification.

* Discard unused result from GetClaimsAsync call

Previously, the result of GetClaimsAsync was assigned to a variable that was never used. Now, the result is explicitly discarded, clarifying that the call is made only for its side effects. No functional changes were made.

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

in IdempotentCacheEntry. On cache hit, re-apply those headers so that
custom headers (e.g. ETag, X-Request-Id) are not lost.

* Initial plan

* Implement Financial & Referral Engine

- Add referralCode, referredById, isActive, walletBalance to User model
- Add GlobalSettings singleton model for configurable fees
- Add Transaction model with type/status enums
- Add AES-256-GCM crypto utility for encrypting Lipana credentials
- Add Lipana.dev payment gateway client (STK push + withdrawal)
- Add wallet, admin server actions with auth guards
- Add Lipana webhook handler: activates users, credits referral bonuses
- Add /wallet, /activate, /withdraw, /referrals, /admin dashboard pages
- Add CopyReferralButton, ActivationForm, WithdrawForm, AdminSettingsForm components
- Add Badge UI component using class-variance-authority
- Add AppSidebarWrapper server component for role-aware sidebar
- Update auth.ts to include role/isActive in JWT/session
- Update register API to accept referralCode and set referredById
- Update register page to read ?ref= param and show referral banner
- Update AppSidebar with Wallet, Referrals, Admin nav items
- Update middleware to protect new routes
- Update dashboard page with wallet balance card
- Add ENCRYPTION_KEY to .env.example

fix: SKU search dropdown now matches single letters like 'D'

Choices.js v10 uses Fuse.js for search which defaults to location-biased
matching (position 0). All SKU labels start with 'Standard_', pushing the
series letter to position 9, causing zero results for single-char searches.

Fix: add fuseOptions to the Choices.js init:
  - ignoreLocation: true  -> search entire string, not just the start
  - minMatchCharLength: 1 -> enable single-character searches
  - threshold: 0.3        -> tighter match quality
  - searchResultLimit: 200 -> show more results (default was 4)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Redis, MinIO with healthchecks and named volumes
- scripts/init-postgres.sh: creates litellm and langfuse databases on boot
- .env.example: full environment variable template (no secrets)

  models, least-busy routing, Langfuse callbacks, Redis caching
- app/main.py: runnable demo (basic chat, tracing, prompt mgmt, cost tracking)

- app/utils/tracing.py: Langfuse SDK wrapper (traces, spans, scoring, prompts)
- app/utils/cost_tracker.py: httpx client for LiteLLM spend/metrics endpoints
- app/examples/: chat_completion, evaluation, prompt_management examples
- tests/: 24 unit tests (fully offline, all mocked), conftest fixtures
- docs/: architecture, configuration, cost-allocation, model-access-management,
  observability, evaluation, prompt-management guides
- README.md: rewritten with architecture diagram, quick start, service table

commit.message:
[Tests][UserEvents] Bump tracee timeout and add logs (#125232)

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/123442

The tracee hit the 5s timeout on the jitstress lane. Given that
jitstress lanes are known to be slow, I'm expecting non-jitstress lanes
to still complete quickly despite the longer timeout. Also added logs to
1) indicate whether the Tracee's EventSource was enabled and 2) reveal
which .NET processes record-trace detected and sent an IPC command.

Below is an example output with these in effect
record-trace emits `Enabled .NET events for process: pid=13803`
Tracee EventSource enabled, emitting events.

record-trace started with PID: 13789
Delaying tracee startup 300ms for record-trace setup...
[record-trace][stdout] 2026-03-05T18:20:38.062008Z  INFO one_collect::helpers::dotnet::os::linux: Enabled .NET events for process: pid=4998
[record-trace][stdout] Recording started.  Press CTRL+C to stop.
[record-trace][stdout] 2026-03-05T18:20:38.068201Z  INFO one_collect::helpers::dotnet::os::linux: Enabled .NET events for process: pid=13767
Starting tracee process: /home/mihw/repo/runtime/artifacts/tests/coreclr/linux.x64.Debug/Tests/Core_Root/corerun /home/mihw/repo/runtime/artifacts/tests/coreclr/linux.x64.Debug/tracing/userevents/custommetadata/custommetadata/custommetadata.dll tracee
Tracee process started with PID: 13803
Waiting for tracee process to exit...
[record-trace][stdout] 2026-03-05T18:20:38.369549Z  INFO one_collect::helpers::dotnet::os::linux: Enabled .NET events for process: pid=13803
[tracee][stdout] Tracee waiting for EventSource to be enabled via IPC...
[tracee][stdout] Tracee EventSource enabled, emitting events.
Stopping record-trace with SIGINT.
Waiting for record-trace to exit...
[record-trace][stdout] 2026-03-05T18:20:38.509142Z  WARN one_collect::helpers::dotnet::os::linux: Failed to open diagnostic socket: pid=13803, nspid=13803
[record-trace][stdout] Recording stopped.
[record-trace][stdout] Resolving symbols.
[record-trace][stdout] Finished recording trace.
[record-trace][stdout] Trace written to /tmp/tmpBl0cAg.nettrace
[record-trace][stdout] 2026-03-05T18:20:38.666852Z  INFO record_trace: record-trace exiting: exit_code=0
CustomMetadata event: Id=1, Name=Item1
Ignored 4441 events from processes other than tracee (PID 13803).

commit.message:
Defer PDF persistence until activity creation; fix filename collisions and download naming (#17)

* Initial plan

* Defer PDF persistence until activity creation, add cache with cleanup, fix naming

- PDFService: cachePdf() stores uploads in-memory instead of persisting immediately
- PDFService: finalizePdf() persists to filesystem + DB when activity is created
- PDFService: getPdfContent/getPdfDocument check cache first, then DB
- PDFService: updatePdfExtractionResults updates cache when PDF not yet finalized
- PDFService: Scheduled cache cleanup every 5min (TTL=60min, max 100 entries)
- PDFService: UUID-prefixed filenames prevent same-name upload collisions
- PDFDocument: Removed @GeneratedValue to allow manual ID from cache key
- ActivityService: createActivityWithValidation calls finalizePdf before creating
- ActivityService: uploadAndCreateActivity uses cache→finalize flow
- ActivityController: getActivityPdf uses activity name as download filename
- DocumentsController: upload endpoint uses cachePdf instead of storePdf
- LearnHubApplication: Added @EnableScheduling for cache cleanup
- Added 10 unit tests for cache operations, finalization, naming, and eviction

Co-authored-by: milljoniaer <78978542+milljoniaer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: sanitize activity name in download filename, rename evict method

Co-authored-by: milljoniaer <78978542+milljoniaer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restore @GeneratedValue on PDFDocument.id; finalizePdf returns JPA-generated ID

The cache key is now purely temporary. finalizePdf() lets JPA auto-generate the
document ID and returns it. createActivityWithValidation() and
uploadAndCreateActivity() use the returned ID as the activity's document_id.

Co-authored-by: milljoniaer <78978542+milljoniaer@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: milljoniaer <78978542+milljoniaer@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve 4 low-priority audit issues (L-06, L-07, L-11, L-13)

L-06: Replace hardcoded '/api/v1/payments/paddle/subscription/cancel' URL
in PlansBillingContent with PAYMENT_ENDPOINTS.SUBSCRIPTION_CANCEL constant.
Add SUBSCRIPTION_CANCEL to PAYMENT_ENDPOINTS in ApiEndpoints.ts.

L-07: Move 'use client' directive to line 1 in resend-verification page
(was on line 4 after comments).

L-11: Bump engines.node from '>=18.0.0' to '>=20.0.0' in root package.json.

L-13: Replace hardcoded getCurrencySymbol() that always returned '$' with
a proper currency symbol map (USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, INR, AUD, CAD, CHF, CNY,
KRW) and fallback to the currency code for unknown currencies.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Authenticate both .npmrc files to fix intermittent npm E401 (#7364)

The build script runs npm ci from two directories, each with its own .npmrc
pointing to the same registry. The existing npmAuthenticate only covered the
task-level .npmrc. The second npm ci (from azure-devops-report/) inherits
TypeScript/.npmrc which was not authenticated, causing intermittent E401s
when agents have stale credentials in their user-level .npmrc.

Add a second npmAuthenticate task for the parent TypeScript/.npmrc so both
npm restores use fresh credentials.

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/extensions/issues/7362

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(backend): use direct Supabase URL with %40-encoded password

URL-encode the @ in the password so url.parse splits correctly:
  postgres:Hydromesh%402025@db.zllbvvgufhhhktaxlpqp.supabase.co:5432/postgres

This was the original ENOTFOUND root cause — the raw @ made url.parse
treat '2025@db...supabase.co' as the host, extracting 'base' as hostname.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Sheets write quota: batch all cell updates in single API call

Push was making 3 API calls per existing row (staff_notes + acknowledged
+ completed), hitting the 60 writes/min quota with 118+ jobs.

Add batch_update_cells() using values().batchUpdate to send all updates
in one request. Also use string TRUE/FALSE instead of batchUpdate
checkbox API to keep everything in the values API.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(wasm): replace Python patch with sed for qwt_plot_spectrogram.cpp guards

Python string replace was failing (likely whitespace/encoding mismatch).
Use sed -i with line-by-line substitution to add #ifndef QWT_NO_CONCURRENT
guards around <qfuture.h> and <qtconcurrentrun.h> includes — more robust
since it matches each include line independently.
Add grep diagnostic to confirm patch applied.

commit.message:
fix: smart flex distribution for ResizablePanel sizing

Previous fix allowed shrinking but panels had flex-grow: 0, so they never
filled available space — causing 100px-wide content areas.

New strategy uses two modes:
- With a flex panel: non-flex siblings stay fixed at defaultSize via
  flex-basis, flex panel fills remaining space (existing behavior)
- Without any flex panel: distribute space proportionally by defaultSize
  ratio (e.g. 15/60/25 → 15%/60%/25% of container)

After drag resize, the effect pins the panel at the new size via
flex-basis, and sibling panels adjust naturally.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Remove trailing whitespace from coverage_test.go (116 lines affected)
- Remove trailing whitespace from error_test.go (82 lines affected)
- Convert octal file permission notation to Go 1.13+ style (0644 → 0o644)

fix(ac-ui): auto-refresh metrics panel every 30s

- Add setInterval(acRefreshIntel, 30000) via _acStartIntelPoll()
- Trigger poll start on project select (acSelectProject) and on load
- Before: metrics fetched once at load, never updated
- After: auto-refresh every 30s, reset on project switch

fix: resolve 18 medium audit findings (M-08..M-30)

- M-08: Remove `as unknown as NextRequest` casts in change-password route
- M-09: Remove 3 dead hooks (useWeeklyTrends, useCheckRank, useAddTag)
- M-10: Add periodic GC to in-memory rate limiter (60s sweep)
- M-11: Replace O(n log n) sort eviction with O(n) linear scan
- M-12: Limit HALF_OPEN concurrent probes in circuit breaker
- M-13: Clear failure history only on CLOSED transition
- M-14: Add maxCacheSize (1000) to FallbackHandler cache
- M-15: FallbackHandler empty strategy now throws instead of `{} as T`
- M-16: FallbackHandler returns `T | Partial<T>` — no more `as T` casts
- M-24: Block private/internal IPs in isValidUrl
- M-25: Block private IPs in extractDomain
- M-26: Replace `as unknown as` in AppConfig with proper Zod parse
- M-28: Fix country risk scoring to compare ISO codes not names
- M-29: ExponentialBackoff validates maxAttempts, proper Error types
- M-30: Add 'use client' to 6 UI forwardRef components

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Configure Aspire MCP server for AI agent integration

- Add ASPIRE_DASHBOARD_MCP_ENDPOINT_URL to launchSettings (http://localhost:16036)
- Add .vscode/mcp.json and .copilot/mcp.json for VS Code and Copilot CLI
- Add scripts/get-aspire-mcp-key.sh helper to retrieve the dynamic API key
- MCP exposes: list_resources, list_console_logs, list_structured_logs,
  list_traces, list_trace_structured_logs, execute_resource_command

After restarting Aspire, the MCP server will be available on HTTP port 16036.
AI agents can query live logs, traces, and resource status across all services.

- Apply syntax highlighting to all terminal code blocks on getting-started
  and devflow pages
